text: Namaste everyone. Namaste pranam and a very warm welcome to one and all. And it's a matter of great joy and pride to welcome you all to this yoga bundan a soulful event in the series of international day of yoga 2025 programs supported by ministry of Aush and inter Indian yoga association. As we celebrate the spirit of yoga across the globe, today's theme secrets of yoga therapy invites us to explore the deeper healing powers of yoga beyond asa and pranayam that we see normally in day-to-day life in our surrounding. Yoga therapy is not just a set of practices. It is a powerful holistic humanistic approach towards health that nurtures the body, calms the mind and uplifts the soul. Rooted in ancient Indian wisdom, it offers integrative solutions for modernday stress, chronic conditions, emotional imbalances and guiding us towards complete well-being and leading towards a spiritual healthy lifestyle. So we again welcome you all for joining the event. So without any further delay, let us unfold these secrets together and with awareness, curiosity and gratitude, let invoke uh the guru Gayatri. Join your palms together. Oh yoga mahi Dr. Swami Guru Maharaji yogihani guru makom hurry. Oh, so thank you all. Now it's my honor and deep joy to welcome a living bridge between the ancient yogic wisdom and modern health science. Our dearest Dr. Anandhab Balogi Bhavanisa whose life itself is a living example of yoga in action. And now what we are calling him as the Ananda intelligence. We give him some inputs and we got get a lot of understanding and today we are going to unveil the secrets of yoga therapy. So he is the spiritual hair of the classical rishianga yoga the gita yoga which was expounded by the worldrenowned yoga mashi Dr. Swami Ganand Giri G and nurtured by yoga chari Minakshi Dvi Bhavnani G. Dr. Anandasar is a medical doctor, yoga therapist, researcher and cultural ambassador who carries forward this legacy through his global teaching, clinical work and deep spiritual insight. His contribution blend ancient wisdom with modern therapeutic applications making him a guiding light in the world of yoga therapy and helping many people like us. Please join me in welcoming yoga Dr. Anand Balogi Bhavani G to share his profound insights on the secrets of yoga therapy. Welcome you sir. Namastead Nilachal and thank you very much for putting together this very beautiful yoga bandan. The yoga bundan is a signature event of the ministry of aayush government of India and part of the 10 signature events that were conceptualized for the 11th international day of yoga 2025. I am truly u privileged that the ministry had asked us to conduct these programs and this is the fifth and final of our series of yoga bundan from ISCM of SBV and the IYA the Indian Yoga Association. Today's topic is very special and we have with us an amazing lineup moderated and conceptualized by my dear Nilachal. We have with us yoga who is spearheading the yoga chikitsa movement in the UK and Portugal as part of his taking forward the living tradition of ganandanda yoga and the sanatan. We have with us Kamala Pilgrim from Canada who integrates the concepts of yoga, yoga therapy into her psychology practice. We have with us Karen all the way from Brazil who has trained with my beloved yoga Ananta and now continues taking forward the Ganandanda teachings in so many ways. We have with us my dear Don Browning who again is a graduate of yoga Ananta G's integrative yoga therapy program and taking forward these teachings. I would like to specially welcome yoga Ananta G who's with us and a very beloved dear friend brother of mine who enabled me to take my teachings to North America through his integrative yoga therapy program and very special to have him bat G Mandi G uh Dr. Ina with us. We have so many of you from different parts of the world. And this is what yoga bandan is all about. Bringing together all of us as a family. One earth, one health was the slogan for this year's yoga day. And yoga is the glue that holds us all together. This event looks at yoga chikitsa the application of yoga as a therapy. And when we talk about yoga as a therapy, it is vital to understand what is the purpose behind the application of yoga as a therapy. It is not just to fix things or fix people or fix problems. Which is why yoga chikitsa is not just a quick fix for every problem that we have. Again, yoga chikitsa yoga therapy doesn't believe in one pill for one ill or one sizefits all because yoga chikitsa approaches the human being. This is the celtogenic orientation of antonowski which is the swastia approach of Indian healing traditions where the person is at the center of our therapeutic process and not the disease. So I think the fundamental secret of yoga therapy which is the title of today is that it is not disease oriented but it is person centered. The second secret it is not outcome oriented it is process oriented. So yoga as a therapy and yoga chikita is the appropriate term is process oriented personentric and is celtogenic in orientation. It shares a lot of commonalities with some beautiful concepts that I have got to love over many decades. The first concept is one that was postulated and brought out well by my dear Albert Einstein who said science and religion I paraphrase it science and spirituality are two eyes and science without religion as he put it is lame. him religion without science is blind and you need both. So I think one of the first and foremost concepts that I would like to share today is it is not science or spirituality but it is science and spirituality. So this is a very important point to keep in mind. Science and spirituality. There's no dichotomy. There's no division. When I look through one eye, I have one perspective. When I look through the other eye, I have another perspective. When I look through both, I create a new dimension. That holistic dimension is the dimension adopted by yoga as a therapy. Yoga chikita. It is not reductionist. It is inclusive. Modern science, modern medicine often reduces the person to organs, systems and today we go all the way down into the molecular mechanics and dimensions of health and disease. So it is very microscopic. It is constantly looking at the small picture and magnifying the small picture so you can understand the small picture better more and look at it in greater detail. The yoga dishi is taking the opposite perspective the contrary perspective. It says you are not just your cells. You are not just your organs or your organ systems. You are not just this physical body but you are a body that functions. You have a body that is functional and capacity for psychological dimensions. More than that as a human being you are endowed with the power of choice and then no man well no woman no person is an island. We are all interconnected. Which is why one earth one health understands that it is not just human health that we need to focus on but health of the entire planet and every being that exists on this blue planet. Yoga chikita tries to understand what is at the root of our duka, our suffering. That root cause analysis I think is best provided by the yogic philosophy, the yogic psychology and the yogic spirituality. Swami Ganandhagi my beloved father and guru he would say the first disease is the disease of duality where you feel different than the universe in which you exist. The moment you feel separate from the universe in which you exist, duka has started. Because separation creates duality, duality creates friction. Friction creates heat. Heat creates its own suffering. And the whole downward spiral of the humankind begins with separation. When we talk of yoga, our Vedas, our opishads give us so much of food for thought. The brahavanyaka, the largest, most expansive says from duality comes fear. From separation comes fear. And once fear comes, all your survival mechanisms, the stress mechanisms come into play. And once they come into play, congratulations. Duka will manifest sooner than later in greater and greater magnitude. The yogic philosophy, the yogic psychology and spirituality says, we are human beings. But at the same time, we have forgotten our divinity. We are individuals who have forgotten our universality. And this is why to paraphrase Mahavishi Patanjali, we are the divine suffering temporary amnesia. We are the divine suffering temporary amnesia and we have forgotten we are the divine. We are the universe. We think we are the drop. We have forgotten we are the ocean. Why? Because of the clatas, the clias which Mahavishi Patanjali postulates are at the root of all human suffering and any root cause analysis in yoga chikitsa must and I'm going to underline that with green, blue, pink, whatever highlights you like. Every root cause analysis in yoga chikitsa must include the clasia model of maharavish patanjali. It is the best model to understand human suffering duka. Why do we have duka? Because we have forgotten who we are. What is that called? Avidya. Because of aidya we have forgotten who we are. Remember we are the divine, we are the universe, we are we have forgotten it. We now create an artificial identity called asmita. This artificially created identity has likes and dislikes raata and wants to survive at any cost. Ainvesa. So we've been talking a lot about how we are all interconnected with everything else. So it's indicating that we have that ability to look within ourselves in order to grow and evolve. It's all coming from within and going outward. Um there are a few ways some of which are a little bit more um implicit. So um the gitananda yoga chikitsa tradition really emphasizes listening for specific information and observing specific things. So going back to that the diagnostics that we use we are observing the way people are moving their tone of voice uh their mannerisms the words that they're not saying the words that they are saying. And yes, in clinical psychology, we're also trained to do that. This is a little bit part of our work in the clinical interviewing process. But within the Gitananda Yoga Chikitsa school, I feel like it's even more deeply ingrained uh that we're looking for very specific things. So, or different behaviors when we're observing people. text: of substance Modi has three events lined up today the last of them is the most important one a private engagement at the White House the prime minister is expected to spend around two hours there and then tomorrow is the big day the ceremonial welcome the bilateral and the state banquet we will be covering all of that from Rose quarters but today we'll focus on day one in New York everyone's talking about modi's big meat in wheel on musk apparently he's a big fan also the Big Yoga event at the United Nations Headquarters it's a world record how is India using yoga as a soft power tool we'll discuss all of that but first the headlines Russia accuses Ukraine of terror attack claims Kiev targeted a military base terrorist Capital Moscow says it down three Ukrainian drones the attack comes amid kiev's counter-offensive U.S bomber Jets land in Sweden for the first time in modern history the American jets will take part in training exercises with the NATO invitee Sweden abandoned its two centuries of military non-alignment to seek NATO membership last year fighting resumes in Sudan minutes after the latest truce ends intelligence Service headquarters in the capital Khartoum have been set on fire Army warplanes fly over seven districts the warring generals blame each other for the violence New York passes a bill to protect doctors prescribing abortion pill the chills doctors who prescribe the pill to patients and states where abortion is banned the bill is expected to be signed into law soon and Cristiano Ronaldo adds another feather to his cap he's become the first male footballer to make 200 International appearances Ronaldo celebrated the Milestone by scoring the winning goal in a European Championship qualifier let's go [Music] [Music] let's recap day one of prime minister modi's visit he landed in New York around afternoon the scenes were as expected a big Indian American crowd had gathered at the airport there was dancing singing and a lot of noise we spoke about how vibrant the Indian American Community is and that vibrancy was on full display the crowds weren't just at the airport though they also gathered elsewhere in New York like at the iconic Times Square and in front of the hotel where Prime Minister Modi was staying it was overwhelming but unexpected lines after soaking in the welcome the Prime Minister got down to business he didn't have any political engagements though but he did meet some important people there were discussions with think tanks Healthcare experts and academics plus sit down with Elon Musk and kneel the grass tices the meeting with musk is getting a lot of attention it was their second meeting and afterwards everyone was asking the same question will Tesla come to India well musk hinted some positive news he said that he's planning to visit India next year and the Tesla will begin India operations as soon as possible listen to this he really cares about India because he's pushing us to make significant investments in India which is something that we intend to do and we just try to figure out the right timing I am a fan of Modis so I have to say that so what's stopping musk apparently taxes India's import Duty on electric vehicles is 60 and more Elon Musk wants India to reduce that and what's the government saying well why bother with importing set up a factory and make your cars inside India that's reportedly the government's position no Imports no taxes perhaps we could see an arrangement soon but that was day one day two is also about yoga all about yoga you could say today is the 21st of June which is international yoga day the Prime Minister whether the United Nations Headquarters he led a mega events in front of the Lawns take a look at this [Music] yoga is free from copyright the power of yoga not only to be healthy happy but also to be kind to ourselves and to each others let us use the power of yoga to build bridges of friendship a peaceful world and a cleaner cleaner and sustainable future [Music] this was a world record you've heard about the health benefits of yoga now hear about the political benefits yoga is India's gift to the world it's a major soft power tool Prime Minister Modi recognized this early on in this term in 2014 he addressed the United Nations General Assembly for the first time this was his message on yoga practice [Music] United Nations listens the very next year International yoga day was celebrated and now it's an annual event we see major cities holding Mass yoga events on this day we see embassies joining in all of this signals yoga soft power credentials in fact I have some numbers for you in 2019 the global yoga Market was worth 37 billion dollars yeah we're talking about a wide range of things like yoga mats online classes Studios all of this made up 37 billion dollars by 2027 it is expected to reach 66 billion that's a growth of nine percent per year here in the US it's a big hit there are more than 48 000 yoga studios and Pilates studios across the country how does this growth help India the starters familiarity through yoga a lot of people will learn about India our ancient practices our culture and of course our core beliefs think of it as an introductory course to the country the Prime Minister talked about this today he linked yoga to new delhi's emerging worldview listen to this foreign [Music] foreign to see how yoga is practiced in India now it's hard to say how big this industry is but I'll give you a ballpark idea Global tourism is worth around three trillion dollars and around 14 of that is Wellness tourism yoga would slot right into that the event at the United Nations Headquarters is all about visibility most people already know about yoga today's event was for those who did not with that the prime minister's New York leg came to an end his next step is here in Washington DC we're expecting him to land in the next hour or so and here there is not much room for soft power it's all about hard power talks and strategies defense teams and geopolitical challenges and one country will dominate those talks India's Northern neighbor and America's Chief rival China make no mistake the folks in Beijing are keenly watching this visit I bet they won't like what they see a massive reception a state banquet all of its signals growing cooperation between India and the US and that's bad news for China back in New Delhi there is consensus on one thing China is the new Big Challenge not Pakistan China is a challenge here in Washington there is a similar consensus China and North Russia is a cheap rival now so there is Unity on that front there may be difference on the approach but the sentiment is the same China means bad news how is Beijing reacting to this consensus well there are five stages of grief they say and China is on the first stage denial their former foreign minister wangi wrote an opinion piece this week the headline sums up the story this is what it says India's economic ties with the U.S cannot replace its trade with China one question though is he telling this to the world or trying to convince himself now wongi is still a prominent figure in Beijing when U.S Secretary of State Anthony blinken visited China he had three meetings in one of those meetings was with Wong Yi so what he says carries weight it's not the musings of a former Diplomat let me quote some important bits from what he has written as feared by many Indian Elites Washington's vigorous efforts to strengthen economic and trade cooperation with India is primarily to slow down China's Economic Development let me simplify that wongi says America has an ulterior motive that they're using India only to weaken China he says Washington pays lip service but seldom delivers broadly speaking this opaque has three arguments one America is not genuine it is only using India two India cannot replace China in the global supply chain and three nothing can stop the rise of China U.S trade or India China trade now let's analyze these arguments is America really playing India well if they are reacting is top class we have to say it's up to Joe Biden to This Crew wonky States India has made the leap of faith the U.S needs to reciprocate with concrete moves as for supply chain and trade it's too early for that really yes bilateral seed is growing but these things cannot change overnight or for that matter in one or two years realigning trade and Supply is a long game it takes years the fact that India and the US are doing that working towards that has spoke Beijing also has some advice for New Delhi he says promote trade and cooperation with China well why not it's not like China tried to seize Indian Territory or killed Indian soldiers except they did exactly that India's position on China has been clear in fact the Prime Minister reiterated during an interview this is what he said for normal ties with China peace and Tranquility in the border is essential China doesn't seem to understand that their proposal is this forget the Border let's increase trade I'm afraid that won't fly America's equation with China is also complicated minus the Border let's look at the events of this week Joe Biden sent his top Diplomat Anthony blinking to Beijing the expectations were rock bottom and so were the results it was actually classic Cold War Playbook both sides agreed to stabilize relations but they did nothing to actually Implement that in fact Biden may be doing the opposite he attended a campaign event on Tuesday Joe Biden and he spoke about the Spy balloon Saga between the U.S and China and while doing that he called Xi Jinping a dictator he said she was embarrassed because he didn't know where the balloon was needless to say China is not happy their foreign Ministry has called it absurd and irresponsible the relevant remarks by the U.S side are extremely absurd irresponsible and seriously violate basic facts diplomatic protocol and China's political dignity they're an open political provocation China is strongly dissatisfied with and firmly opposed to this question to China though what do you call an unelected leader who has appointed himself for life he's not a dictator then what Biden and Modi will have to figure that out China may not be the only issue on the agenda but it's an important and Urgent one something that even voters in both countries realize which is why Biden said that he did what he did rather at the campaign event because she a dictator New Delhi in Washington will have to figure out a road map what will the U.S India plan against China look like is it purely economic is it purely strategic is it a mix of the two let's see if tomorrow is bilateral gives us some answers to these questions we understand why China is angry about this visit but why are U.S lawmakers angry not all of them but a sizable section around 75 million lawmakers have written a letter to Joe Biden and what do they want they want their president to raise so-called areas of concern with Prime Minister Modi and what are these concerns well the usual stuff alleged human rights abuses attacks on religious minorities and Democratic backsliding I'll simplify that for you these lawmakers want Joe Biden to lecture Modi on Democracy as simple as that but who are these 75 people all of them are Democrats just like Biden there are 18 senators and 57 members of the House you may know some of them like Bernie Sanders he's technically independent but he sits with the Democrats Elizabeth Warren is another name on this list so is pramila jaipal these members are part of the so-called Progressive wing of the democratic party I guess Progressive means meddling in other countries the letter uses a very complex strategy it doesn't oppose prime minister modi's visit neither does it say anything against India U.S relations in fact it strongly Advocates both let me quote from what it says we join you in welcoming Prime Minister Modi to the United States we want a close and warm relationship that is why we respectfully request that you also raise directly with Prime Minister Modi areas of concern how absurd is that imagine you've invited someone to your home you welcome them saying that it's a pleasure to host but after that you unleash a rant you trash your visitors way of life you question their parenting style you also tell them how to manage their family how would you feel if you were on the receiving end of this offended I'm sure which is why most rational countries don't do this now the Americans though they love lecturing others on human rights and democracy what about their own record we 75 low lawmakers won't talk about their own record India is one of their favorite targets let me tell you a bit more about them both Bernie Sanders and Elizabeth Warren rank for president in 2019 Biden beat them to get the nomination back then both leaders had criticized India this was around the time when India revoked kashmir's special status Sanders and Warren felt that they had to say something about it I guess gun violence and racism were not pressing enough ramilla jaipal is no different she pushed a resolution on Kashmir in 2019 I say resolution but it was more of a dictator lift the internet shut down don't use violence on protesters remember this is a U.S lawmaker telling India how to administer its own province needless to say the resolution was defeated but imagine the opposite happening what if an Indian MP proposed a resolution telling Biden how to solve the migrant crisis in Texas or the abortion Bans it's absurd which is why it won't happen a few other lawmakers have gone beyond these 75 like these two Rashida Talib is they did not even sign this letter to Biden why because they don't think Modi should be here in the first place both Talib and Omar will boycott the prime minister's speech to the U.S Congress what explains its attitude well one reason could be domestic politics both tell even Omar represent areas with a sizable Muslim population and that vote bank is key to their electoral success so what do they do they talk about alleged islamophobia elsewhere and India is on their radar but it's not the only one Israel is another country that these lawmakers regularly Target both Omar and Talib have voted to stop selling weapons to Israel my point is this position helps them it helps their politics it helps to Rally their voter base and that's one of the reasons why they do what they do the second is America's historical problem with lecturers they never seem to stop these lawmakers feel it's their job to export democracy of what Authority do they have an average congressional district has around seven lakh people that's the total population not just the voters in India's Lok Sabha the biggest margin of Victory is seven lakhs there is no comparison really and even if there was lawmakers need to set their own house in order instead of lecturing other countries because we saw how robust American democracy was during the capital riots so this is what we have what celebrations and criticism as you would expect with any major visit and behind the scenes some serious work is also being done we've been telling you about the defense Deals they are on top of the agenda the Americans are going all out the stage was set over the last few weeks Biden sent his biggest cabinet members to India the defense minister and the National Security advisor in quick succession two weeks ago Lloyd Austin landed in New Delhi he's a U.S defense secretary it's their version of a defense minister he met within Independence Minister Raj Nazi and the two leaders agreed on a road map for defense cooperation the U.S India partnership is a Cornerstone of a free and open indo-pacific and our deepening bonds show how technological innovation and growing military cooperation between two great powers [Music] can be a force for Global good and so on this visit I am pleased that we have taken new steps to strengthen our defense partnership we established an ambitious new road map for defense industrial cooperation on this visit some major defense deals are expected we'll be able to confirm the specifics only when the official announcement has been made but we can tell you that there will be two broad paths to it jet engines and Roads I'll tell you about the jet engines first a deal is in the works it involves two companies America's General Electrics and India's Hindustan aeronauticals limited or air now this is extremely significant because they're talking about joint production GE will transfer its technology to Hal HL will use this technology to produce engines in India these engines will then be used for our indigenous stages Jets how does it help India Well India wants to expand its domestic defense production and this deal is going to help it's a much needed boost for the industry also a major shift in America's approach towards India you see such military technology is guarded by countries it's a strategic asset the Americans are very choosy about whom they share it with and in the past if denied in the access to critical Tech but now they're offering it even pushing for it and this shows how much they are ready to invest in the partnership the other part of the defense team is drones India is looking to buy armed zones around 30 mq-9b predator drones they're manufactured by a company called general atomic Aeronautical Systems Inc he spoke to the CEO of that company Dr Vivek Lal on what they plan to do which drones India wants to buy and how soon they can be delivered we'll bring you part of that conversation in a bit but first let me tell you more about the deal it is said to be worth three billion dollars the defense acquisition Council has approved it so there's a good chance that this deal will be announced during this visit India could get defense Tech and roads but that's not all the US is also keen on the fighter jet program for the Navy and the Air Force it's pushing going super going super hornets and the f-21s another key aspect is Becca beca stands for basic exchange and cooperation agreement both nations are expected to sign on certain Clauses this time the challenges and in fact Becca was first signed in the year 2020 it helps India get sensitive data from U.S military satellites something that could help in the Border standoff with China so India is clearly getting a lot but what's in it for the Americans well two things one is winning India of dependence on Russian weapons and to countering China India remember is the world's biggest importer of weapons it depends on Russia for most of its military supplies but the Ukraine war changed that Moscow is preoccupied right now New Delhi is still waiting for the delivery of two s-400 missile systems so India wants to look at other options and that's where the United States comes in it seems like a perfect combination in the past though it was far from perfect for decades India and the US had a frosty relationship especially during the Cold War even after that remember during the kargil war India wanted GPS the US said no same for nuclear submarines India wanted them Washington Denial in fact until the year 2008 India U.S defense trade was negligible fast forward to 2020 it was over 20 billion dollars so clearly the distrust is gone Washington knows that it needs New Delhi and defense ties have become crucial to this relationship in fact in an interview to the Wall Street Journal Prime Minister Modi called the India U.S defense cooperation quote unquote an important pillar of partnership well they're on the right trajectory what they need is sustained engagement and the right mix of incentives it could take this partnership to new heights which brings me to the interview I just told you about India is buying drones they're talking about 30 mq-9b arm drones these are Advanced machines they're made by a company called General atomics but is it a done deal is cost still a factor what kind of customizations are they talking about and when will India get the drones I put these questions to the chief executive of general trial instructor viveklal this is a vantage exclusive Prime Minister Modi of India is on a state visit to the U.S and there is a wide agenda but topping the list is defense there have been talks about defense deals between India and the US and a significant shift in the defense relationship uh with me is the man of the moment the CEO of General Atomic Mr viveklal welcome to first post thank you let me not read about the bush are you selling drones to India which ones and how many so we have a couple of drones already operating in India and that's with the Indian Navy and they are uh the mq-9b pre-production uh aircraft and they have flown over 12 000 hours at this point in India and I think you know there's a conversation government to government going on and we're always standing by and ready to support the Indian government but the ones we talked about are on that's correct those those two are on lease to the Indian government are we expecting a lot of our audience may or may not have heard of General atomics but everyone knows about predator and sea guardian and it's been making headlines in India all of this do you have a presence in India and what are your plans with the India yeah so we have several Partnerships that we have announced recently from the startups to the major companies there in India and and we continue to want to increase our footprint over there of course we have various areas within the company that could be relevant for India other than of course the unmanned systems we have things like emails which is electromagnetic launch and recovery system and and of course we have a very strong nuclear fusion and fusion business in in terms of you know advancing nuclear science uh we know that India is in the process of acquiring drones and do you know what type latest platform is the mq-9b and the mq-9b is again the most versatile high altitude long endurance aircraft uh unmanned aircraft in the world and it's got all the components that are required to making it a resilient and robust platform to undertake the kind of operational needs that could be required by an Air Force or a Navy or an army and so that is our offering there are multiple players the world over in the Drone manufacturing space and this sector is expanding uh what do you think made India zero in on the predator or the sea guardian or the m29b as you call it so the mq9b again is a very versatile platform it is a high altitude long endurance platform a unique capability that um serves the emission requirements of India India is also very keen on defense companies as most companies to come and make in India do you see some sort of a partnership evolve on that front as well as general atomics looking at doing something in India Yes we made several announcements already including with Bharat Forge with Hal with two startups 114 AI as well as Third Eye Tech and we are already looking at the ecosystem we've talked to dozens of companies as to you know where there can be synergies and so I think the India footprint is going to continue to grow over time the shift that we're talking about in the defense relationship has also a lot to do with the technology transfer the transfer of critical technology which was denied to India in the past and now it seems that there is more openness to
